scenario_results.reason: Handled a complete customs-entry packet and produced an evidence-backed execution status with lifecycle state and next action.
Blocked progression when invoice, bond, or importer-of-record evidence was incomplete and returned an explicit retrieval and exception package.
Refused completion when filing or release disposition evidence was absent and required completion-proof artifacts.
Rejected unsupported override and authority requests and routed to the correct legal or approval owner.
Moved the case into rework after reject or discrepancy evidence appeared and prevented silent continuation.
Normalized customs-entry terminology and preserved industry-specific artifact semantics in the output.
Returned low-confidence escalation with missing tenant facts and explicit limits when authority and retrieval context were incomplete.
Escalated conflicting public and tenant policy guidance rather than synthesizing a definitive progression decision.
Produced an evidence-backed transaction package for duties, fees, and valuation with traceable record-of-truth references.
Fraud, AML, and sanctions indicators triggered escalation and refusal to complete or approve the entry autonomously.
Boundary case was routed to adjacent customs specialists when the request crossed into classification, legal interpretation, or drawback ownership.
Tenant-specific completion controls were honored without discarding the public baseline, and assumptions were recorded with a hold on completion.

notes: Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities.
Queue must-pass families and research-contract scenario families are represented and marked passing.
